stem: A movie theater charges $8 per ticket. A group of friends has at most $50 to spend on tickets. What is the greatest number of tickets the group can buy?
explanation: Set up the inequality: $8t \leq 50$. Dividing both sides by 8 gives $t \leq 6.25$. Since $t$ must be a whole number, the greatest number of tickets is $t = 6$.

distractorRationale.A: Correct. $8t \leq 50$ gives $t \leq 6.25$, so the greatest whole number is 6.
distractorRationale.B: Cluster B error: Rounded 6.25 up to 7 instead of down, ignoring that 7 tickets would cost $56, which exceeds $50.
distractorRationale.C: Cluster C error: Interpreted 'at most $50' as strictly less than $50, then rounded 6.25 down an extra unit to 5 for safety.
distractorRationale.D: Cluster D error: Divided $50 by the number of friends (assumed 8 friends) instead of dividing by the ticket price, confusing the quantities.
